messages.content: Doing so, I can't write more than one character without re-selecting the form to continue writing.
more easy way?

Apologies for any confusion earlier. Here's a simpler and more straightforward method to achieve the same goal without using debouncing. You can update the parent state only when the `MyBlock` component is blurred (i.e., when the focus is lost).

Modify the `MyBlock` component in `AddComponents.jsx`:

For the textarea:

```javascript
<Form.Control as="textarea" value={content} onChange={(ev) => setContent(ev.target.value)} onBlur={() => props.updateBlockContent(x, content)}></Form.Control>
```

For the radio input:

```javascript
<input value={image.url} onChange={(ev) => setContent(ev.target.value)} onBlur={() => props.updateBlockContent(x, content)} checked={content == image.url} type="radio" />
```

Now, the parent component's state will be updated only when the focus is lost from the input elements in the `MyBlock` component. This approach allows for seamless editing and only updates the parent component when necessary.
Nice solution but if I am writing and I press the handle up button nothing happens because only onBlur is called and not the button I pressed

Warning: Maximum update depth exceeded. This can happen when a component calls setState inside useEffect, but useEffect either doesn't have a dependency array, or one of the dependencies changes on every render.
not working
Too difficult. I need a very much simple way to handle content reflection.

I apologize for the complexity of earlier solutions. A simple approach to reflecting content is to use a two-way data binding method with a local state and update the parent component's state when the focus is lost.

Here's a simplified solution:

1. In `AddComponents.jsx`, change the `onChange` event handlers to only set the local content:

For the textarea:

```javascript
<Form.Control as="textarea" value={content} onChange={(ev) => setContent(ev.target.value)} />
```

For the radio input:

```javascript
<input value={image.url} onChange={(ev) => setContent(ev.target.value)} checked={content == image.url} type="radio" />
```

2. In `AddComponents.jsx`, add an onBlur event on both input fields for updating the parent component's state:

For the textarea:

```javascript
<Form.Control as="textarea" value={content} onChange={(ev) => setContent(ev.target.value)} onBlur={() => props.updateBlockContent(x, content)} />
```

For the radio input:

```javascript
<input value={image.url} onChange={(ev) => setContent(ev.target.value)} onBlur={() => props.updateBlockContent(x, content)} checked={content == image.url} type="radio" />
```

This way, the parent component will be updated only when you lose focus on the input field, and you can work with the handle up and handle down buttons without any conflict. This approach is simpler and keeps the component structure fairly straightforward.
it's an amazing solution but if I am writing inside the form and then I press handle up, handle up doesn't work because it triggers onblur condition
yes but like that when I handleup only the content of x block is reflected and not the other block I am moving
too difficult. I want the changes to be reflected when I press anything else than the box
so When I click the handle up button it will trigger both onblur and handle up?

Yes, you're right. Upon clicking the handle up button, both the `onBlur` event of the `div` and the `handleUp` in the `MyBlock` component will be triggered. The `onBlur` event will update the parent state with the current content, while the `handleUp` function is responsible for changing the order of the blocks.

This ensures that the changes to the content will be reflected in the parent state when clicking outside of the textarea or when using the handleUp button, thus achieving the desired behavior.
It does not trigger both two. When I click the handle up button it triggers only the onblur, I have to click a second time to handle up!
Too difficult. Rethink the whole process and please provide me changes for a better solution
if I change block 1 order to block 2, to only one of the 2 blocks the changes will be reflected
How can I update a state without triggering the re-render?

You cannot update a state in React without triggering a re-render because React is designed to re-render a component whenever its state updates. This is the core concept of React's reactive nature.

However, if you wish to store a value without triggering a re-render, you can use `useRef`.

`useRef` creates a mutable object with a `current` property. Unlike state updates, when the `current` property is changed, it does not cause a re-render of the component. It is useful for storing values without provoking a re-render or for instance in retaining DOM elements across renders.
